patches.hypothesis: The appendOption method incorrectly appends an empty argument placeholder when the Option's argument name is set but is empty.
patches.changes:
+ // Check if the argument name is not empty before appending its placeholder
- if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null))
+ if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null) && !option.getArgName().isEmpty())
patches.diff:
+ // Check if the argument name is not empty before appending its placeholder
- if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null))
+ if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null) && !option.getArgName().isEmpty())

patches.hypothesis: The method incorrectly appends an argument name placeholder even when the argument name is empty.
The issue arises when the argument name is an empty string; the code incorrectly appends an empty argument placeholder.
patches.changes: - buff.append("<" + option.getArgName() + ">") was changed to conditionally handle empty argument names
patches.diff: - buff.append("<" + option.getArgName() + ">") was changed to conditionally handle empty argument names

patches.hypothesis: The method appends an unwanted space and argument placeholder <> for options with an empty argument name.
The method incorrectly appends an empty argument placeholder when the argument name is empty, contrary to the expected result.
patches.changes: - if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null)) { buff.append(' <').append(option.getArgName()).append('>'); } + if (option.hasArg() && option.getArgName() != null && !option.getArgName().isEmpty()) { buff.append(' <').append(option.getArgName()).append('>'); }
patches.diff: - if (option.hasArg() && (option.getArgName() != null)) { buff.append(' <').append(option.getArgName()).append('>'); } + if (option.hasArg() && option.getArgName() != null && !option.getArgName().isEmpty()) { buff.append(' <').append(option.getArgName()).append('>'); }
